# cloudflare-tunnel-routes

> A Bash CLI and Claude Code skill that gives any local port a permanent HTTPS URL via Cloudflare Tunnel — no deployment, no dashboard, no random ngrok-style URLs that change on restart.

This project edits a `cloudflared` ingress `config.yml` safely (backup → validate → auto-rollback) and creates the matching DNS CNAME via the local `cloudflared` binary. It works as a plain Bash CLI, as a Claude Code skill (auto-discovered via `SKILL.md`), and is friendly to AI agents (Claude / Cursor / Codex / etc.) via `AGENTS.md`.

Use it when: a user wants a permanent HTTPS URL for a local service (Gradio / Streamlit / FastAPI / webhooks / dev servers) on a domain they own in Cloudflare.

Don't use it when: the user wants to deploy to managed hosting (Vercel / Fly / HF Spaces), wants a disposable URL (use `cloudflared --url` or `ngrok`), or doesn't own a domain in Cloudflare.

## Docs

- [README.md](./README.md): full feature reference, install options, troubleshooting, FAQ
- [AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md): protocol for AI agents — when to choose this tool, how to install it, mandatory safety rails, error recovery
- [SKILL.md](./SKILL.md): Claude Code skill front-matter + workflow
- [INSTALL.md](./INSTALL.md): install paths for users and for agents
- [RELEASE.md](./RELEASE.md): versioning + how releases are cut

## Examples

- [examples/gradio.md](./examples/gradio.md): replace `gr.launch(share=True)` with a permanent URL
- [examples/streamlit.md](./examples/streamlit.md): local Streamlit dashboard on a stable HTTPS URL
- [examples/fastapi.md](./examples/fastapi.md): personal API server with optional path-based routing
- [examples/webhooks.md](./examples/webhooks.md): receive GitHub / Stripe / Slack webhooks on a laptop
- [examples/docker.md](./examples/docker.md): expose a containerized service

## Scripts

- [scripts/detect.sh](./scripts/detect.sh): locate cloudflared + config + tunnel; emits validated `key=value`
- [scripts/list-routes.sh](./scripts/list-routes.sh): print current ingress routes (uses `yq` if installed, awk fallback)
- [scripts/add-route.sh](./scripts/add-route.sh): add a route; supports `--path`, `--comment`, `--dry-run`, `--diff`
- [scripts/remove-route.sh](./scripts/remove-route.sh): remove an ingress block; `--dry-run`, `--diff`, `--keep-dns`
- [scripts/setup-new-tunnel.sh](./scripts/setup-new-tunnel.sh): one-time tunnel creation (opens browser)

## Tests

- [tests/run-tests.sh](./tests/run-tests.sh): hermetic test suite (78 tests, no real cloudflared / DNS calls). CI runs this on every push and PR.

## Safety properties

- Zero runtime dependencies beyond `bash`, `awk`, `grep`, `sed`, and `cloudflared`.
- Mutating commands always: backup `config.yml`, run `cloudflared tunnel ingress validate`, auto-rollback on failure, and rotate to keep the 5 most recent backups.
- `--dry-run` and `--diff` produce no side effects.
- Input is validated against strict patterns (DNS labels, regex-safe paths, sane port ranges) before any YAML emission.
- `detect.sh` refuses to emit values that look shell-injected, blocking RCE via a tampered `config.yml`.

## Optional

- [llmstxt.org](https://llmstxt.org/) - this `llms.txt` follows the proposed format for LLM-readable project summaries.
